seo.title: Building Code Generation with Llama Stack + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with Llama Stack + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Llama Stack provides a standardized Python interface for building sophisticated Llama-powered agents with built-in safety, memory, and tool execution capabilities, making it ideal for code generation workflows. Integrating Vex guardrails ensures generated code is safe, syntactically valid, and free from credential leaks before execution. This combination delivers reliable, production-ready code generation while mitigating common agentic failure modes.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with Llama Stack
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Llama Stack code generation architecture features an agent loop where the LLM receives a coding task, generates code using its reasoning capabilities, and leverages tools for sandbox execution and testing. Memory bank stores previous iterations and test results, while safety shields filter outputs. The agent iterates until tests pass or limits are reached.

Key components include the LlamaStackClient for agent orchestration, sandbox tools for secure execution, and structured memory retrieval for context. Vex verification wraps each agent turn to validate code safety and correctness before tool execution.

This setup handles iterative refinement automatically, with the agent self-correcting based on test feedback while Vex ensures no malicious patterns emerge during refinement.

risks.name: Malicious code injection
risks.description: Agent generates code with system compromise patterns, file deletion, or network attacks
risks.mitigation: Vex code safety scanner blocks execution of dangerous patterns before sandbox

risks.name: Credential leakage
risks.description: Generated code embeds API keys, passwords, or secrets from memory/context
risks.mitigation: Vex credential detector scans outputs and redacts sensitive patterns

risks.name: Unsafe dependency injection
risks.description: Code installs/executes untrusted packages via pip/os.system
risks.mitigation: Vex dependency whitelist blocks unauthorized package operations

risks.name: Infinite execution loops
risks.description: Agent generates recursive code that exceeds sandbox timeouts
risks.mitigation: Vex execution bounds checker validates loop structures pre-execution

implementation.title: Initialize Llama Stack Client
implementation.description: Set up the LlamaStack client with sandbox tools and memory configuration for code generation
implementation.code:
from llama_stack_client import LlamaStackClient
import vex

# Initialize Llama Stack with code generation tools
client = LlamaStackClient(
    model="llama-3.1-70b",
    tools=[
        "code_sandbox",
        "pytest_runner",
        "file_validator"
    ],
    memory_config={"max_context": 128000}
)

# Configure Vex for high-risk code generation
vex.configure(profile="codegen-high-risk")

implementation.title: Core Agent Loop with Sandbox
implementation.description: Main agent loop that generates, tests, and refines code iteratively

implementation.title: Test-Driven Refinement
implementation.description: Agent uses test failures to iteratively improve code quality
implementation.code:
def refine_with_tests(code: str, test_results: dict):
    feedback = f"""
Previous code failed tests:
{test_results}

Refine the code to fix these issues:
1. {test_results['failures'][0]['error']}
2. Ensure all tests pass.
    """
    
    refinement_prompt = f"FIX THIS CODE: {code}\nFEEDBACK: {feedback}"
    refined_response = client.conversations.say(refinement_prompt)
    
    return refined_response.content
